characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Good Parents: Julia, Mary Jo and Charlene are shown to be loving and attentive parents, and although the others don't have children,they nonetheless have a good rapport with children in general. True Companions: The quartet (along with Anthony and Bernice) are close friends to the point of being like family.

characters.name: Suzanne
characters.description: Big Beautiful Woman: The later seasons had her put on weight, but she never lost her grace or style. The Fashionista: She's usually seen in various high fashion outfits. Gold Digger: Implied and outright stated by her that some of her spouses she only had interests in their money (although granted, they were only interested in her looks). It's All About Me: She has a high sense of self and she tends to come off as arrogant and self-centered. I Was Quite a Looker: Provoked: after she is body-shamed by her former classmates at her reunion, she believes that she's no longer considered as beautiful as she was in her youth.

characters.name: Julia
characters.description: Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: Even through their very opposite personalities and clashes, she and Suzanne do love each other dearly. Cool Big Sis: To Suzanne and the others in general. She's quick to defend them and give them sound advice. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: She is the level-headed and mature sister to Suzanne's impulsive and flighty. Happily Married: She was this to her belated husband (who died prior to the show). Practically Different Generations: She's much older than Suzanne and practically raised her.

characters.name: Mary Jo
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: One of her standout qualities. Her and Suzanne often trade barbs back and forth. Fiery Redhead: Sheisa character played by Annie Potts, so it's only fitting that she's one. The Rival: The earlier episodes has her sharing an enmity with Suzanne and they often snarked at each other, but they soon became good friends. Single Woman Seeks Good Man: Even after a bitter divorce, she still believes in love and wants to find Mr. Right. What, Exactly, Is Her Job?: While it's stated that she is the main designer of the firm, given herOne-Hour Work Weekand being moreso seen making observations and quips, you have to wonder how extensive her role is.

characters.name: Charlene
characters.description: Country Mouse: Having lived a much sheltered life than her friends, many of her small-town ideals are the source of humor. The Ditz: Downplayed. She's not quite at the level ofRose, but she's far less sophisticated than her friends and often misses the point. Elvis Lives: One episode has her encountering an Elvis lookalike in the form of a hitchhiker, causing her to briefly believe this. Fan Girl: She's a huge one ofElvis Presleyandcountry music in general(with her celebrity guardian angel being none other thanDolly Parton!) Happily Married: Eventually becomes this to Bill, her boyfriend, a Colonel in the Air Force.

characters.name: Anthony
characters.description: Odd Friendship: An exceptionally odd one with Suzanne, who has said and done racist things in his presence and even to him. The One Guy: Even through the revolving door cast and a host of boyfriends, husbands, flings and others, he remains the main (and only) male amongst several female characters. Unlucky Everydude: Downplayed, but he certainly doesn't have the best luck in life, especially whenever he's aroundSuzanne. Unwanted Spouse: Played with: he and a Vegas showgirl named Eitenne end up getting married and try to make it work, but ultimately decided they're too incompatible, although they did split more or less amicably.

characters.name: Bernice
characters.description: Cloudcuckoolander: Her defining trait. Eventually her eccentricities are believed to be an actual mental problem, requiring a court-mandated sanity hearing. Drop-In Character: She always manages to come by at random, but is nonetheless welcome.
